name: Algerian Couscous
recipeIngredient: 4 small yellow squash or yellow zucchini
4 medium yellow or red potatoes, skins on
recipeInstructions:
1. In a pot, sauté onion in vegetable stock over medium-low heat until translucent.
2. Add all spices and cook for a few more minutes, stirring as needed.
3. Stir in tomato paste and simmer for 2 minutes.
4. Cut the vegetables into large chunks (excluding garbanzo beans) and add them to the pot with a dash of cinnamon. Add water to cover the ingredients. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered, for an hour or so. Cooking time can extend up to 2-3 hours.
5. About 5 minutes before removing the vegetables from heat, add the drained garbanzo beans.
6. In a separate bowl, pour boiling water over couscous and let it sit for about 5 minutes. Fluff with a fork (using a ratio of approximately 1.5:1 of water to couscous). For added flavor, mix some of the liquid from the vegetable stew into the couscous instead of using all water.
7. Serve the stew over the couscous and enjoy!
